records.display_name: Q72 afternoon bus bunching: up to 45 min wait

payload.claim_text: Bus bunching in the afternoon often results in a wait as long as 45 minutes for the bus to pick passengers up at the terminal
payload.subject: Q72 bus bunching

records.display_name: Only 7% of passengers arrive by bus

payload.claim_text: Only seven percent of passengers are arriving at LaGuardia by bus
payload.subject: LaGuardia Airport passenger access

records.display_name: Q72: 25 min AM, 45 min PM from Queens Blvd to LGA

payload.claim_text: Q72 Commuter: It is quick during the morning hours – 25 minutes from Queens Boulevard to the LGA. But Junction Boulevard is very congested in the afternoon. It can take 45 minutes to get from LGA back to Queens Boulevard during the afternoon hours.
payload.subject: Q72 travel time

records.display_name: M60 bus bunching and crowding

payload.claim_text: The M60 often has bus bunching, with very long gaps between buses. Conditions on board are very crowded.
payload.subject: M60 service quality

records.display_name: Preferred BRT features: Real Time Info, Enhanced Stations, Offset Bus Lanes, Off-Board Fare

payload.claim_text: The participants preferred Real Time Bus Information, Enhanced Stations, Off-set Bus Lanes and Off-Board Fair Collection. They did not prefer Curbside Bus Lanes.
payload.subject: BRT feature preferences

records.display_name: Majority of NYC passengers come from Manhattan

payload.claim_text: A majority of passengers (whose trip begins in New York City) are coming from Manhattan, followed by Brooklyn and Queens
payload.subject: LaGuardia Airport passenger origins

records.display_name: Majority supported BRT or supported with concerns

payload.claim_text: Asked how they now felt about BRT as a solution to access LaGuardia Airport, a majority of the participants either supported it or supported with concerns
payload.subject: Workshop participants' view on BRT

records.display_name: BRT selected as locally preferred mode

payload.claim_text: The decision was made to move forward with Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) as the locally preferred mode since it fulfilled the three primary goals of the study: Improve Transit Mobility, Connectivity, and Accessibility to LaGuardia Airport; Improve Transit Service to Neighborhoods; Provide Cost Effective and Efficient Transport Options in a Short-term Timeframe
payload.subject: LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis

records.display_name: Most interest in route between airport and 74th St/Roosevelt Ave

payload.claim_text: When asked which neighborhood could benefit the most from a new BRT service, the route between the airport and the 74th Street/Roosevelt Avenue station through Jackson Heights drew the most interest
payload.subject: BRT route preference

records.display_name: Majority arrive by hired car or personal car

payload.claim_text: A majority of travelers arrive by hired car (such as taxi or black car) or van service, followed by personal cars
payload.subject: LaGuardia Airport passenger access modes

records.display_name: LaGuardia Airport Access Alternatives Analysis

description: Study of transit access alternatives to LaGuardia Airport funded by FTA grant received December 2010. Alternatives Analysis began in May 2011. Focus on low-cost improvements within a short timeframe. Led by NYCDOT working with MTA New York City Transit, MTA Bus Company, Port Authority of NY & NJ, and NYSDOT. Uses FTA Alternatives Analysis process to qualify for future FTA funding. Multiple modal alternatives considered: Bus Rapid Transit, Light Rail Transit, Ferry, Automated Guideway Transit, Streetcar, Heavy Rail. Connects LGA to commercial hubs in Astoria, Harlem, The Hub (Bronx), Midtown, East Elmhurst/Jackson Heights, and Flushing.
Study examining Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) as the locally preferred mode for improving transit access to LaGuardia Airport

Study begun in May 2011 to develop faster and more reliable transit service to LaGuardia Airport. Identified Bus Rapid Transit (BRT) as the best-suited transit mode under the Select Bus Service (SBS) brand name.

payload.description: Study of transit access alternatives to LaGuardia Airport funded by FTA grant received December 2010. Alternatives Analysis began in May 2011. Focus on low-cost improvements within a short timeframe. Led by NYCDOT working with MTA New York City Transit, MTA Bus Company, Port Authority of NY & NJ, and NYSDOT. Uses FTA Alternatives Analysis process to qualify for future FTA funding. Multiple modal alternatives considered: Bus Rapid Transit, Light Rail Transit, Ferry, Automated Guideway Transit, Streetcar, Heavy Rail. Connects LGA to commercial hubs in Astoria, Harlem, The Hub (Bronx), Midtown, East Elmhurst/Jackson Heights, and Flushing.
